name: Honolulu Museum of Art
tagline: Hawaii's largest art museum — 50,000 works spanning 5,000 years, housed in a stunning 1927 building with open-air courtyards.
parking: Street parking on Beretania St or paid lots nearby. Limited museum lot
bestTime: First Friday of the month — free admission, live music, food trucks, and a festive atmosphere. Weekday mornings are quietest
kamaaina: Free admission for Hawaii residents with valid ID. General admission $20
talkStory: Founded in 1927 by missionary descendant Anna Rice Cooke, who believed 'that the people of Hawaii should be able to look at great art.' The building was designed by Bertram Goodhue Associates in a Hawaiian-Mediterranean style with open-air courtyards that let the trade winds flow through the galleries. Its Asian art collection is considered one of the finest in the US, including a rare Hokusai 'Great Wave' print and works spanning 5,000 years. First Fridays mean free admission, live music, and food trucks — the best deal in Honolulu's art scene. Also operates Shangri La; book that separately.
